---
title: "Comparing Robust ANOVA Methods under Heteroscedasticity: A simulation study of Welch, Brown-Forsythe, and Permutation Tests"
author: "The Vobian"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```

```{r}
#library(car) #for Welch's and Brown-Forsythe. This package "car" is for for quality of variances.
#I later installed the package "onewaytests" which performs a heteroscedastic robust test for equality of means
# Install once (you can add to your setup chunk)
# install.packages("onewaytests")


```

```{r}
# Load necessary packages

library(tidyverse)#dplyr,ggplot2,tidyr
library(onewaytests) # for Brown-Forsythe (means) and Welch

```

```{r}

set.seed(123)# for keeping the random numbers generated same each time the code is run
#let's generate an unbalanced, heteroscedastic normal data n(10,20,30) with variance (1,4,9)
sample_1<-rnorm(10, mean=0,sd=sqrt(1)) #small sample with low varance
sample_2<-rnorm(20, mean=0,sd=sqrt(4)) #medium sample with medium varance
sample_3<-rnorm(30, mean=0,sd=sqrt(9)) #large sample with high varance

#dataframe to handle the samples above
sample_df<-data.frame(
  value=c(sample_1, sample_2, sample_3),
  samples = factor(rep(c("1","2","3"), times=c(10,20,30)))
)
sample_df

```


```{r}
#Boxplot to visualize group distribution. this confirms heteroscedascity (unequal variances)
boxplot(value ~ samples, data = sample_df,
        main = "Boxplot of Value by samples",
        xlab = "Sample",
        ylab = "Value",
        col = c("skyblue3", "red3", "green3"),
        border = "darkgray")
```



```{r}
#Test 1: Classical ANOVA
classic<-aov(value~samples,data = sample_df)
summary(classic)
cat("Classic ANOVA p-value:",summary(classic)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1],"\n")
```


```{r}
#Test 2: Welch's test: adjustments for unequal variances
Welch<-oneway.test(value~samples, data = sample_df, var.equal = FALSE)
summary(Welch)
Welch$statistic
cat("Welch summary is as follows:\n","Welch Statistic:",Welch$statistic,"\n",
    "Welch Parameter:",Welch$parameter,"\n","Welch P-value:",Welch$p.value,"\n",
    "Welch Method:",Welch$method,"\n","Welch data.name:",Welch$data.name,"\n"
    )

```


```{r}
#Test 3: Brown-forsythe mean test
# Single run example (replace your current bf line)
bf_result <- bf.test(value ~ samples, data = sample_df)

# Extract just the p-value
p_bf <- bf_result$p.value
# Extract just the test statistic
stat_bf <- bf_result$statistic
cat("Brown-Forsythe Statistic:", stat_bf, "\n")
cat("Brown-Forsythe P-value:", p_bf, "\n")
```


```{r}
#Test 4: Permutation test function for the Normal distribution
n_perm <- 99

obs_F <- summary(aov(value ~ samples, data = sample_df))[[1]][["F value"]][1]

perm_F <- replicate(n_perm, {
  shuffled <- sample(sample_df$value)
  df_perm <- data.frame(value = shuffled,
                        samples = sample_df$samples)
  anova(lm(value ~ samples, data = df_perm))[1, "F value"]
})

perm_p <- (sum(perm_F >= obs_F) + 1) / (n_perm + 1)
obs_F #print the F statistic
perm_F #Print the different permutations
perm_p #print the p value
```


```{r}
#p value for all four tests
cat("Classic ANOVA p-value:",summary(classic)[[1]]$Pr[1],"\n",
"Welch P-value:",Welch$p.value,"\n",
"Brown-Forsythe P-value is :",p_bf,"\n",
"Permutation P-value is:",perm_p,"\n")
```


```{r}
#visualizing the Permutation distribution
perm_dist<-replicate(n_perm,{summary(aov(sample(value)~samples, data = sample_df))[[1]]$F[1]})
obs_F<-summary(aov(value~samples, data = sample_df))[[1]]$F[1]
hist(perm_dist,breaks=30, main="Permutation Distribution of F", xlab="F-Statistic", col="lightblue2")
abline(v=obs_F, col="red",lwd=2)
legend("topright","Observed F", col="red", lwd = 2)
```




```{r}
# Function for handling p value for the 4 ANOVA methods with normal distribution
set.seed(123)

run_tests <- function(values, groups) {
  df <- data.frame(value = values, samples = as.factor(groups))
  
  # Classic ANOVA
  p_classic <- summary(aov(value ~ samples, data = df))[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]
  
  # Welch's ANOVA
  p_welch <- oneway.test(value ~ samples, data = df, var.equal = FALSE)$p.value
  

  # Brown-Forsythe (suppress verbose output)
p_bf <- {
  tmp <- capture.output(res <- bf.test(value ~ samples, data = df, na.rm = TRUE))
  res$p.value
}

  
  # Permutation
  n_perm <- 99

obs_F <- anova(lm(value ~ samples, data = df))[1, "F value"]

perm_F <- replicate(n_perm, {
  shuffled <- sample(df$value)
  df_perm <- data.frame(
    value = shuffled,
    samples = df$samples
  )
  anova(lm(value ~ samples, data = df_perm))[1, "F value"]
})

p_perm <- (sum(perm_F >= obs_F) + 1) / (n_perm + 1)
  
  return(c(p_classic, p_welch, p_bf, p_perm))
}
```


```{r}

# Simulate Type I Error for normal

simulate_type1 <- function(n1, n2, n3, sd1, sd2, sd3, dist = "normal", n_sim = 100) {
  p_vals <- matrix(NA, nrow = n_sim, ncol = 4)
  
  for (i in 1:n_sim) {
    if (dist == "normal") {
      s1 <- rnorm(n1, mean = 0, sd = sd1)
      s2 <- rnorm(n2, mean = 0, sd = sd2)
      s3 <- rnorm(n3, mean = 0, sd = sd3)
    }
    else if (dist == "lognormal") {
  # Target a specific mean so all groups are identical despite different sdlog
             target_mean <- 5 
  
 # Calculate meanlog (mu) for each group
             mu1 <- log(target_mean) - (sd1^2)/2
             mu2 <- log(target_mean) - (sd2^2)/2
             mu3 <- log(target_mean) - (sd3^2)/2
  
             s1 <- rlnorm(n1, meanlog = mu1, sdlog = sd1)
             s2 <- rlnorm(n2, meanlog = mu2, sdlog = sd2)
             s3 <- rlnorm(n3, meanlog = mu3, sdlog = sd3)
             }
    else if (dist == "t") {
      s1 <- rt(n1, df = 3)
      s1 <- s1 / sqrt(3)
      s1 <- s1 * sd1

      s2 <- rt(n2, df = 3)
      s2 <- s2 / sqrt(3)
      s2 <- s2 * sd2

      s3 <- rt(n3, df = 3)
      s3 <- s3 / sqrt(3)
      s3 <- s3 * sd3
    }
    
    values <- c(s1, s2, s3)
    groups <- c(rep("1", n1), rep("2", n2), rep("3", n3))
    p_vals[i, ] <- run_tests(values, groups)
  }
  
  colnames(p_vals) <- c("Classic", "Welch", "Brown-Forsythe", "Permutation")
  
  #calculate type 1 error rates
  type1_error_rates<-apply(p_vals < 0.05, 2, mean)
  return(list(type1_error_rates=type1_error_rates, p_values=p_vals))}

# Run simulations for normal
type1_balanced_equal_n <- simulate_type1(20, 20, 20, sqrt(1), sqrt(1), sqrt(1), dist = "normal")
type1_unbalanced_unequal_n <- simulate_type1(10, 20, 30, sqrt(1), sqrt(4), sqrt(9),dist = "normal")
type1_balanced_unequal_n <- simulate_type1(15, 15, 15, sqrt(1), sqrt(4), sqrt(9),dist = "normal")
type1_unbalanced_equal_n <- simulate_type1(5, 10, 15, sqrt(1), sqrt(1), sqrt(1),dist = "normal")

# Measure execution time
#execution_time <- system.time({
 # type1_balanced_equal <- simulate_type1(20, 20, 20, sqrt(1), sqrt(1), sqrt(1))
  #type1_unbalanced_hetero <- simulate_type1(10, 20, 30, sqrt(1), sqrt(4), sqrt(9))
  #type1_balanced_hetero <- simulate_type1(15, 15, 15, sqrt(1), sqrt(4), sqrt(9))
  #type1_unbalanced_equal <- simulate_type1(5, 10, 15, sqrt(1), sqrt(1), sqrt(1))})
  
# Combine results for normal
results_n <- data.frame(
 Scenario = c("Balanced Equal Var","Unbalanced Unequal Var","Balanced Unequal Var","Unbalanced Equal Var"),
  rbind(type1_balanced_equal_n$type1_error_rates,type1_unbalanced_unequal_n$type1_error_rates,
        type1_balanced_unequal_n$type1_error_rates,type1_unbalanced_equal_n$type1_error_rates)
)

#fixing columns names
colnames(results_n)[2:5] <- c("Classic",
                              "Welch",
                              "Brown-Forsythe",
                              "Permutation")
cat("The type1 error rates for Normal are as follows\n")
print(results_n)
#cat("\nTotal run time:\n")
#print(execution_time)

#making sure type one error rates is ready for plotting
type1_errors1 <- pivot_longer(
  results_n,
  cols = c("Classic", "Welch", "Brown-Forsythe", "Permutation"),
  names_to = "Method",
  values_to = "ErrorRate1"
)
# names under bars should match the order
type1_errors1$Scenario <- factor(
  type1_errors1$Scenario,
  levels = c("Balanced Equal Var","Unbalanced Unequal Var","Balanced Unequal Var","Unbalanced Equal Var")
)

```


```{r}
# Run simulations for lognormal
type1_balanced_equal_ln <- simulate_type1(20, 20, 20, sqrt(1), sqrt(1), sqrt(1), dist = "lognormal")
type1_unbalanced_unequal_ln <- simulate_type1(10, 20, 30, sqrt(1), sqrt(4), sqrt(9),dist = "lognormal")
type1_balanced_unequal_ln <- simulate_type1(15, 15, 15, sqrt(1), sqrt(4), sqrt(9),dist = "lognormal")
type1_unbalanced_equal_ln <- simulate_type1(5, 10, 15, sqrt(1), sqrt(1), sqrt(1),dist = "lognormal")

# Combine results for lognormal
results_ln <- data.frame(
   Scenario = c("Balanced Equal Var","Unbalanced Unequal Var","Balanced Unequal Var","Unbalanced Equal Var"),
   
  rbind(type1_balanced_equal_ln$type1_error_rates,type1_unbalanced_unequal_ln$type1_error_rates,
        type1_balanced_unequal_ln$type1_error_rates,type1_unbalanced_equal_ln$type1_error_rates)
)
#fixing columns names
colnames(results_ln)[2:5] <- c("Classic",
                              "Welch",
                              "Brown-Forsythe",
                              "Permutation")
cat("The type1 error rates  for Lognormal are as follows\n")
print(results_ln)

#making sure type one error rates is ready for plotting
type1_errors2 <- pivot_longer(
  results_ln,
  cols = c("Classic", "Welch", "Brown-Forsythe", "Permutation"),
  names_to = "Method",
  values_to = "ErrorRate2"
)

# names under bars should match the order
type1_errors2$Scenario <- factor(
  type1_errors2$Scenario,
  levels = c("Balanced Equal Var","Unbalanced Unequal Var","Balanced Unequal Var","Unbalanced Equal Var")
)

```


```{r}
# Run simulations for t
type1_balanced_equal_t <- simulate_type1(20, 20, 20, sqrt(1), sqrt(1), sqrt(1), dist = "t")
type1_unbalanced_unequal_t <- simulate_type1(10, 20, 30, sqrt(1), sqrt(4), sqrt(9),dist = "t")
type1_balanced_unequal_t <- simulate_type1(15, 15, 15, sqrt(1), sqrt(4), sqrt(9),dist = "t")
type1_unbalanced_equal_t <- simulate_type1(5, 10, 15, sqrt(1), sqrt(1), sqrt(1),dist = "t")
# Combine results for t
results_t <- data.frame(
   Scenario = c("Balanced Equal Var","Unbalanced Unequal Var","Balanced Unequal Var","Unbalanced Equal Var"),
   
  rbind(type1_balanced_equal_t$type1_error_rates,type1_unbalanced_unequal_t$type1_error_rates,
        type1_balanced_unequal_t$type1_error_rates,type1_unbalanced_equal_t$type1_error_rates)
)
#fixing columns names
colnames(results_t)[2:5] <- c("Classic",
                              "Welch",
                              "Brown-Forsythe",
                              "Permutation")
cat("The type1 error rates  for t are as follows\n")
print(results_t)

#making sure type one error rates is ready for plotting
type1_errors3 <- pivot_longer(
  results_t,
  cols = c("Classic", "Welch", "Brown-Forsythe", "Permutation"),
  names_to = "Method",
  values_to = "ErrorRate3"
)

# names under bars should match the order
type1_errors3$Scenario <- factor(
  type1_errors3$Scenario,
  levels = c("Balanced Equal Var","Unbalanced Unequal Var","Balanced Unequal Var","Unbalanced Equal Var")
)
```

 

```{r}
#creating a type1error plot for the Normal, lognormal and t

# Plot1 for normal
plot1<-ggplot(type1_errors1, aes(x = Scenario, y = ErrorRate1, fill = Method)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.05, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", size = 1, ) +
  annotate(geom = "label",x=0.8,y=0.05+0.02,label="Nominal value=0.05", color="red", size=3)+
  labs(title = "Comparison of Type I Error Rates for normal",
       subtitle = "Across ANOVA Methods Under Different Conditions",
       y = "Type I Error Rate", x = "Design Scenario") +
 
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.4, size = 9, color="blue3",vjust = 8),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size=16, hjust = 0.5),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 14, hjust=0.5),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size=16),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size=16),
        legend.position = "top")
ggsave("type1error_normal.tiff",plot1)
plot1


#plot 2 for lognormal
plot2<-ggplot(type1_errors2, aes(x = Scenario, y = ErrorRate2, fill = Method)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.05, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", size = 1) +
  annotate(geom = "label",x=0.8,y=0.05+0.01,label="Nominal value=0.05", color="red", size=3)+
  labs(title = "Comparison of Type I Error Rates for Lognormal",
       subtitle = "Across ANOVA Methods Under Different Conditions",
       y = "Type I Error Rate", x = "Design Scenario") +
  theme_minimal() +
   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.4, size = 9, color="blue3",vjust = 8),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size=16, hjust = 0.5),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 14, hjust=0.5),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size=16),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size=16),
        legend.position = "top")
ggsave("type1error_lognormal.tiff",plot2)
plot2 


#plot 3 for t distribution
plot3<-ggplot(type1_errors3, aes(x = Scenario, y = ErrorRate3, fill = Method)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.05, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", size = 1) +
  annotate(geom = "label",x=0.8,y=0.05+0.04,label="Nominal value=0.05", color="red", size=3)+
  labs(title = "Comparison of Type I Error Rates for t",
       subtitle = "Across ANOVA Methods Under Different Conditions",
       y = "Type I Error Rate", x = "Design Scenario") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.4, size = 9, color="blue3",vjust = 8),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size=16, hjust = 0.5),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 14, hjust=0.5),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size=16),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size=16),
        legend.position = "top")
ggsave("type1error_t.tiff",plot3)

plot3 
```
 

#Power analysis simulation 



```{r}
#Power analysis simulation 

# General simulation function for power analysis
simulate_power <- function(n1, n2, n3, sd1, sd2, sd3, means, dist = "normal", n_sim = 100) {
  p_vals <- matrix(NA, nrow = n_sim, ncol = 4)
  
  for (i in 1:n_sim) {
    if (dist == "normal") {
      s1 <- rnorm(n1, mean = means[1], sd = sd1)
      s2 <- rnorm(n2, mean = means[2], sd = sd2)
      s3 <- rnorm(n3, mean = means[3], sd = sd3)
    } else if (dist == "lognormal") {
      mu1 <- log(means[1]) - (sd1^2)/2
      mu2 <- log(means[2]) - (sd2^2)/2
      mu3 <- log(means[3]) - (sd3^2)/2

      s1 <- rlnorm(n1, meanlog = mu1, sdlog = sd1)
      s2 <- rlnorm(n2, meanlog = mu2, sdlog = sd2)
      s3 <- rlnorm(n3, meanlog = mu3, sdlog = sd3)
    } else if (dist == "t") {
      s1 <- rt(n1, df = 3)
      s1 <- s1 / sqrt(3)
      s1 <- s1 * sd1 + means[1]
      
      s2 <- rt(n2, df = 3)
      s2 <- s2 / sqrt(3)
      s2 <- s2 * sd2 + means[2]
      
      s3 <- rt(n3, df = 3)
      s3 <- s3 / sqrt(3)
      s3 <- s3 * sd3 + means[3]
    }
    
    values <- c(s1, s2, s3)
    groups <- c(rep("1", n1), rep("2", n2), rep("3", n3))
    p_vals[i, ] <- run_tests(values, groups)
  }
  
  colnames(p_vals) <- c("Classic", "Welch", "Brown-Forsythe", "Permutation")
  apply(p_vals < 0.05, 2, mean)
}

# Power wrapper for Normal distribution
evaluate_normal_power <- function(n_sim = 100) {
  list(
    Balanced_Equal     = simulate_power(20, 20, 20, 1, 1, 1, c(0, 0.5, 1), "normal", n_sim),
    Unbalanced_Unequal = simulate_power(10, 20, 30, 1, sqrt(4), sqrt(9), c(0, 0.5, 1), "normal", n_sim),
    Balanced_Unequal   = simulate_power(15, 15, 15, 1, sqrt(4), sqrt(9), c(0, 0.5, 1), "normal", n_sim),
    Unbalanced_Equal = simulate_power(5, 10, 15, 1, 1, 1, c(0, 0.5, 1), "normal", n_sim)
    
  )
}

# Power wrapper for Lognormal distribution
evaluate_lognormal_power <- function(n_sim = 100) {
  list(
    Balanced_Equal     = simulate_power(20, 20, 20, 1, 1, 1, c(5, 6, 7), "lognormal", n_sim),
    Unbalanced_Unequal = simulate_power(10, 20, 30, 1, sqrt(4), sqrt(9), c(5, 6, 7), "lognormal", n_sim),
    Balanced_Unequal   = simulate_power(15, 15, 15, 1, sqrt(4), sqrt(9), c(5, 6, 7), "lognormal", n_sim),
    Unbalanced_Equal = simulate_power(5, 10, 15, 1, 1, 1, c(5, 6, 7), "lognormal", n_sim)
  )
}

# Power wrapper for t-distribution
evaluate_t_power <- function(n_sim = 100) {
  list(
    Balanced_Equal     = simulate_power(20, 20, 20, 1, 1, 1, c(0, 0.5, 1), "t", n_sim),
    Unbalanced_Unequal = simulate_power(10, 20, 30, 1, sqrt(4), sqrt(9), c(0, 0.5, 1), "t", n_sim),
    Balanced_Unequal   = simulate_power(15, 15, 15, 1, sqrt(4), sqrt(9), c(0, 0.5, 1), "t", n_sim),
    Unbalanced_Equal = simulate_power(5, 10, 15, 1, 1, 1, c(0, 0.5, 1), "t", n_sim)
  )
}

# Normal distribution power
evaluate_normal_power(100)


# Lognormal distribution power
evaluate_lognormal_power(100)

# t-distribution power
evaluate_t_power(100)

```



```{r}
#creating a statistical Power plot for the Normal

# Create a data frame of your results : Normal
# Combine power results correctly

normal_power_results <- evaluate_normal_power(100)
power_normal <- data.frame(
  Scenario = c("Balanced Equal Var","Unbalanced Unequal Var","Balanced Unequal Var","Unbalanced Equal Var"),
  rbind(normal_power_results$Balanced_Equal,
        normal_power_results$Unbalanced_Unequal,
        normal_power_results$Balanced_Unequal,
        normal_power_results$Unbalanced_Equal)
)

# Rename columns PROPERLY
colnames(power_normal)[2:5] <- c("Classic",
                                 "Welch",
                                 "Brown-Forsythe",
                                 "Permutation")

cat("The power rates for Normal are as follows\n")
print(power_normal)

# Now pivot
power_normal_long <- pivot_longer(
  power_normal,
  cols = c("Classic", "Welch", "Brown-Forsythe", "Permutation"),
  names_to = "Method",
  values_to = "Power"
)

# Fix scenario order
power_normal_long$Scenario <- factor(
  power_normal_long$Scenario,
  levels = c("Balanced Equal Var","Unbalanced Unequal Var","Balanced Unequal Var","Unbalanced Equal Var")
)

```


```{r}
# Create a data frame of your results : logNormal

# Combine power results correctly

normal_ln_power_results <- evaluate_lognormal_power(100)
power_normal_ln <- data.frame(
  Scenario =c("Balanced Equal Var","Unbalanced Unequal Var","Balanced Unequal Var","Unbalanced Equal Var"),
  rbind(normal_ln_power_results$Balanced_Equal,
        normal_ln_power_results$Unbalanced_Unequal,
        normal_ln_power_results$Balanced_Unequal,
        normal_ln_power_results$Unbalanced_Equal)
)

# Rename columns PROPERLY
colnames(power_normal_ln)[2:5] <- c("Classic",
                                 "Welch",
                                 "Brown-Forsythe",
                                 "Permutation")

cat("The power rates for LogNormal are as follows\n")
print(power_normal_ln)

# Now pivot
power_normal_ln_long <- pivot_longer(
  power_normal_ln,
  cols = c("Classic", "Welch", "Brown-Forsythe", "Permutation"),
  names_to = "Method",
  values_to = "Power"
)

# Fix scenario order
power_normal_ln_long$Scenario <- factor(
  power_normal_ln_long$Scenario,
  levels = c("Balanced Equal Var","Unbalanced Unequal Var","Balanced Unequal Var","Unbalanced Equal Var")
)

```


```{r}
#creating a statistical Power plot for t
# Create a data frame of your results : t

# Combine power results correctly
t_power_results <- evaluate_t_power(100)
power_t <- data.frame(
  Scenario = c("Balanced Equal Var","Unbalanced Unequal Var","Balanced Unequal Var","Unbalanced Equal Var"),
  rbind(t_power_results$Balanced_Equal,
        t_power_results$Unbalanced_Unequal,
        t_power_results$Balanced_Unequal,
        t_power_results$Unbalanced_Equal)
)

# Rename columns PROPERLY
colnames(power_t)[2:5] <- c("Classic",
                                 "Welch",
                                 "Brown-Forsythe",
                                 "Permutation")

cat("The power rates for t are as follows\n")
print(power_t)

# Now pivot
power_t_long <- pivot_longer(
  power_t,
  cols = c("Classic", "Welch", "Brown-Forsythe", "Permutation"),
  names_to = "Method",
  values_to = "Power"
)

# Fix scenario order
power_t_long$Scenario <- factor(
  power_t_long$Scenario,
  levels = c("Balanced Equal Var","Unbalanced Unequal Var","Balanced Unequal Var","Unbalanced Equal Var")
)

```


```{r}
# Plot1 for normal
power_plot1<-ggplot(power_normal_long, aes(x = Scenario, y = Power, fill = Method)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.80, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", size = 1, ) +
  annotate(geom = "label",x=1,y=0.82,label="Target Power=0.80", color="red", size=3)+
  labs(title = "Comparison Statistical Power for normal",
       subtitle = "Across ANOVA Methods Under Different Conditions",
       y = "Statistical Power", x = "Design Scenario") +
 
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.4, size = 9, color="blue3",vjust = 8),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size=16, hjust = 0.5),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 14, hjust=0.5),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size=16),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size=16),
        legend.position = "top")
ggsave("power_normal.tiff",power_plot1)
power_plot1


#plot 2 for lognormal
power_plot2<-ggplot(power_normal_ln_long, aes(x = Scenario, y = Power, fill = Method)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.80, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", size = 1, ) +
  annotate(geom = "label",x=1,y=0.82,label="Target Power=0.80", color="red", size=3)+
  labs(title = "Comparison Statistical Power for Lognormal",
       subtitle = "Across ANOVA Methods Under Different Conditions",
       y = "Statistical Power", x = "Design Scenario") +
 
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.4, size = 9, color="blue3",vjust = 8),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size=16, hjust = 0.5),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 14, hjust=0.5),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size=16),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size=16),
        legend.position = "top")
ggsave("power_lognormal.tiff",power_plot2)
power_plot2

#plot 3 for t distribution
power_plot3<-ggplot(power_t_long, aes(x = Scenario, y = Power, fill = Method)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.80, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", size = 1, ) +
  annotate(geom = "label",x=1,y=0.82,label="Target Power=0.80", color="red", size=3)+
  labs(title = "Comparison Statistical Power for t",
       subtitle = "Across ANOVA Methods Under Different Conditions",
       y = "Statistical Power", x = "Design Scenario") +
 
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.4, size = 9, color="blue3",vjust = 8),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size=16, hjust = 0.5),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 14, hjust=0.5),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size=16),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size=16),
        legend.position = "top")
ggsave("power_t.tiff",power_plot3)
power_plot3

```
end of simulation

```{r}
#testing if Rcpp works with C++
library(Rcpp)
evalCpp("1+1")
```
